checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are peers penalized for sending over-cap transactions?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Configure a test peer to repeatedly send transactions with gasLimit > 16777216. 2. Monitor peer score changes on the receiving node. 3. Verify the peer is eventually disconnected after threshold violations. 4. Test that legitimate peers are not penalized for occasional invalid transactions. 5. Review peer scoring configuration for appropriate thresholds.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-19, Trust Boundaries: tb-002, tb-009. Peer flooding protection.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is ModExp multiplication_complexity >= 16 for all inputs?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Call ModExp with various base_length and modulus_length combinations. 2. Verify complexity is 16 for max_length <= 32 and >= 50 for max_length > 32. 3. Test edge cases: max_length = 0, 1, 32, 33. 4. Search for any input that produces complexity < 16. 5. Compare gas charged across all major clients.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-20, Trust Boundaries: tb-007, tb-008. Complexity floor prevents DoS.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is complexity exactly 16 when max_length <= 32?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Call ModExp with max(base_length, modulus_length) = 32. 2. Verify multiplication_complexity = 16. 3. Call with max_length = 33 and verify complexity = 50. 4. Test the exact boundary condition across all clients. 5. Document any deviations from expected values.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-21, Trust Boundary: tb-007. Boundary inconsistency causes consensus failure.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is complexity formula correct for max_length > 32 with overflow protection?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify formula: complexity = 2 * ceil(max_length/8)^2 for max_length > 32. 2. Test with large base_length/modulus_length values near integer limits. 3. Verify no integer overflow in words^2 computation. 4. Use static analysis to check for overflow-safe arithmetic. 5. Test with inputs designed to trigger overflow.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-22, Trust Boundary: tb-007. Overflow wraps gas cost enabling DoS.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is iteration_count exactly 1 when exponent is 0?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Call ModExp with exponent = 0 and exponent_length <= 32. 2. Verify iteration_count = 1 (not 0). 3. Verify gas cost is max(500, complexity * 1) = 500 for small inputs. 4. Test across all major Ethereum clients. 5. Check for division-by-zero or other edge case issues.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-23, Trust Boundary: tb-007. Zero exponent edge case.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is iteration_count = bit_length(exponent) - 1 for non-zero exponent <= 32 bytes?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Call ModExp with various exponent values (1, 2, 255, 256, 2^32-1). 2. Verify iteration_count = bit_length(exponent) - 1 for each. 3. Test power-of-2 boundaries where bit_length changes. 4. Compare results across all major clients. 5. Document any bit_length calculation differences.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-24, Trust Boundary: tb-007. Cross-client consistency required.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is large exponent multiplier 16 (not 8 from EIP-2565)?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Call ModExp with exponent_length = 64 (> 32). 2. Calculate expected gas: 16 * (64-32) + bit_length(top_32_bytes). 3. Verify actual gas matches (multiplier 16, not 8). 4. Compare gas across all clients for the same inputs. 5. Alert if any client uses multiplier 8.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-25, Trust Boundary: tb-007. EIP-7883 uses 16, not EIP-2565's 8.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is 16*(exponent_length-32) overflow-protected?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Calculate max safe exponent_length: uint64_max / 16 + 32. 2. Call ModExp with exponent_length near overflow boundary. 3. Verify no integer overflow occurs (gas remains very large, not wrapped to small). 4. Review code for overflow-checked arithmetic. 5. Test with various integer type implementations.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-26, Trust Boundary: tb-007. Overflow enables free expensive operations.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is final gas = max(500, complexity * iterations) without /3?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Call ModExp with known inputs and calculate expected gas. 2. Verify formula: max(500, floor(complexity * iterations)). 3. Explicitly verify NO division by 3 is applied. 4. Compare gas across all clients. 5. Alert if any client's gas is 3x lower than expected (indicating /3 still present).
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-27, Trust Boundary: tb-008. EIP-7883 removes the /3 divisor.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is minimum gas floor 500 (not 200)?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Call ModExp with minimal inputs producing complexity * iterations < 500. 2. Verify exactly 500 gas is charged. 3. Verify NOT 200 gas (old EIP-2565 minimum). 4. Test across all clients. 5. Calculate maximum calls per transaction at new vs old minimum.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-28, Trust Boundary: tb-007. Higher minimum limits attack amplification.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is ModExp gas cost deterministic and identical across all clients?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Create a comprehensive test vector suite covering all formula paths. 2. Execute identical ModExp calls on all major clients. 3. Compare gas consumed for each test case. 4. Alert on any divergence. 5. Include edge cases, boundary values, and large inputs.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-29, Trust Boundary: tb-008. Gas divergence breaks consensus.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is all ModExp arithmetic overflow-protected (256-bit or arbitrary precision)?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Review ModExp gas calculation code for integer types used. 2. Verify ceil, words^2, 2*words^2, 16*(exp_len-32) use safe arithmetic. 3. Test with inputs near uint64 and uint256 boundaries. 4. Use fuzzing to find overflow-inducing inputs. 5. Document precision requirements for each intermediate value.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-30, Trust Boundaries: tb-007, tb-008. Comprehensive overflow protection required.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is complexity * iterations product overflow-protected?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Find input combinations where both complexity and iterations are large. 2. Calculate theoretical product and verify no overflow. 3. Test with base_length near max and exponent_length near max. 4. Verify gas remains very large (exceeds transaction limit) for such inputs. 5. Review code for checked multiplication.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-31, Trust Boundaries: tb-007, tb-008. Product overflow enables free execution.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is memory allocation bounded before EIP-7823 limits applied?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Call ModExp with very large base_length or modulus_length values. 2. Monitor memory allocation before gas check. 3. Verify memory limits are enforced before allocation. 4. Test with base_length = 2^32 and insufficient gas. 5. Verify no OOM or crash occurs.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-32, Trust Boundaries: tb-007, tb-008. OOM can crash validator nodes.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is max_length 32/33 branch boundary identical across clients?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Call ModExp with max_length = 32 on all clients; verify complexity = 16. 2. Call with max_length = 33 on all clients; verify complexity = 50. 3. Document exact branch condition in each client's code. 4. Alert on any <= vs < confusion at boundary. 5. Test both base_length = 32 and modulus_length = 32 variations.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-33, Trust Boundary: tb-007. Branch inconsistency causes consensus failure.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is there NO division by 3 in the EIP-7883 formula?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Review ModExp gas calculation code for any /3 or div(3) operations. 2. Call ModExp and compare actual gas with formula with and without /3. 3. Verify actual gas matches complexity * iterations (not complexity * iterations / 3). 4. Cross-client comparison to detect /3 presence. 5. Alert if any client's gas is exactly 1/3 of expected.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-34, Trust Boundary: tb-008. Most critical EIP-7883 change.

checklist_item.title: Is the minimum 500 gas floor always applied when computed gas < 500?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Find all input combinations where complexity * iterations < 500. 2. For each, verify exactly 500 gas is charged. 3. Test small operands: base_length=1, mod_length=1, exp_length=1, exp=0. 4. Verify no path bypasses the max(500, ...) check. 5. Document any inputs that produce < 500 gas.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-35, Trust Boundaries: tb-007, tb-008.

checklist_item.title: Is ModExp execution time bounded and proportional to gas charged?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Benchmark ModExp with various inputs measuring CPU time vs gas charged. 2. Plot time/gas ratio across input space. 3. Identify any outliers with disproportionately high time/gas ratio. 4. Test worst-case inputs at maximum allowed gas. 5. Verify total block processing time remains acceptable.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-36, Trust Boundaries: tb-007, tb-008.

checklist_item.title: Is block processing time acceptable with maximum ModExp calls at minimum gas?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Deploy a contract making repeated minimum-gas ModExp calls. 2. Execute transaction with gasLimit = 16777216. 3. Measure total execution time for ~33,554 ModExp calls. 4. Verify execution completes within acceptable attestation window. 5. Test on representative validator hardware.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-37, Trust Boundary: tb-007.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does ModExp check gas availability BEFORE executing?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Call ModExp with insufficient gas for the operation. 2. Verify the call reverts immediately without executing the computation. 3. Monitor CPU usage to confirm no significant computation occurred. 4. Review code to verify gas check order: parse -> compute cost -> check -> allocate -> execute. 5. Test with just-below-sufficient gas.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-38, Trust Boundary: tb-008.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is iteration_count >= 1 for all inputs?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Test inputs designed to minimize iteration_count. 2. Verify max(iteration_count, 1) is applied. 3. Check large exponent path: 16*(exp_len-32) + bit_length(top_32) >= 1. 4. Test with top_32_bytes = 0 to minimize bit_length contribution. 5. Verify no zero-cost execution path exists.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-39, Trust Boundaries: tb-007, tb-008.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does ModExp handle all edge case inputs deterministically?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Test edge cases: base_length=0, modulus_length=0, exponent_length=0. 2. Test all-zero inputs and single-byte inputs. 3. Test max_length at 32/33 boundary. 4. Test very large length values. 5. Execute on all major clients and compare gas and results.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-44, Trust Boundary: tb-007.

checklist_item.title: Is gas = max(500, 16*1) = 500 for smallest ModExp inputs?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Call ModExp with max_length <= 32 and exponent = 0. 2. Verify: complexity = 16, iterations = 1, gas = max(500, 16) = 500. 3. Test across all clients. 4. This is the absolute minimum gas for any ModExp call. 5. Document any deviation.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-45, Trust Boundaries: tb-007, tb-008.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are P2P messages always independently validated regardless of peer reputation?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Establish a peer with good reputation through normal behavior. 2. Switch the peer to sending over-cap transactions. 3. Verify each transaction is still validated and rejected. 4. Review code for any cached validation shortcuts based on peer. 5. Test that no 'trusted peer' bypass exists.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-46, Trust Boundaries: tb-002, tb-009.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is ModExp gas computed before memory allocation?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Call ModExp with large lengths and minimal gas. 2. Monitor memory allocation during the call. 3. Verify NO memory is allocated before gas check fails. 4. Review code order: parse lengths -> compute gas -> check gas -> allocate. 5. Test with lengths designed to trigger OOM if allocation came first.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-47, Trust Boundary: tb-008.
